summary: The test suite provides reasonable coverage of core functionality—parsing (JUnit XML and JSON), aggregation, flaky test detection, markdown rendering, and workflow integration via act. Tests are well-organized with clear describe blocks and helpful assertions on exact expected values (e.g., ::AGG:: status lines). However, rigor is moderate: unit tests (aggregator.test.ts) are shallow, covering happy paths but missing most error cases (invalid status values, malformed JSON, missing required fields, file I/O failures). The three act-driven integration tests verify specific real-world scenarios with precise assertions, which is good, but edge cases and boundary conditions are sparse (no tests for very large datasets, special characters, empty inputs, or graceful degradation). Design is strong—temp directories, git repo setup, and helper functions (setupRepo, runAct, assertAct) are well-abstracted and independent—but the heavy reliance on 5-minute act timeouts creates slow feedback. Fixture files (run-1-junit.xml, run-2-junit.xml, run-3-results.json) are referenced but content not shown; assuming they exist, they'd strengthen confidence. Would catch major regressions (parsing breaks, aggregation logic errors) but likely miss edge cases and error-handling gaps.
